title: Lines, Sent To A Gentleman Whom He Had Offended.
content:
The friend whom wild from wisdom's way,
            The fumes of wine infuriate send;
        (Not moony madness more astray;)
            Who but deplores that hapless friend?

        Mine was th' insensate frenzied part,
            Ah, why should I such scenes outlive
        Scenes so abhorrent to my heart!
            'Tis thine to pity and forgive.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Robert Burns delivers a powerful performance in "Lines, Sent To A Gentleman Whom He Had Offended."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
